fault_hypothesis: On line 67, the overlap condition uses `<=` and `>=` instead of `<` and `>`, causing touching intervals (where end == s or start == e) to be incorrectly treated as non-overlapping.

spec.expr: not (end__1 < s or start__1 > e) == (not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e))

constraints.intent: The condition using < and > should produce the same result as <= and >= only when intervals do not touch at boundaries; this is FALSE when end==s or start==e (the failing cases).
Verify that end is strictly greater than start before using it in the overlap check.

spec.expr: not (end__1 < s or start__1 > e) or (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e)

constraints.intent: If the correct strict-inequality check detects a conflict, the buggy <= / >= check must also detect it (but not vice versa); this is FALSE when end==s or start==e and the correct check fires but the buggy one does not.

spec.expr: all(b[0] >= a[1] for a, b in zip(updated__1, updated__1[1:]))

constraints.intent: After inserting the new interval, every consecutive pair in the sorted schedule must be strictly non-touching (no boundary contact), which would be violated when a touching interval is incorrectly inserted.
